sections.instruction: What is said about using each of the following hotel facilities? Choose THREE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to Questions 8-10.
sections.passage_or_context:
Availability
A included in cost of hiring room
B available at extra charge
C not available

questions.content:
(8) outdoor swimming pool
(9) gym
(10) tennis courts
options: A included in cost of hiring room
B available at extra charge
C not available

sections.instruction: What information does the speaker give about each of the following excursions? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 11-16.
sections.passage_or_context:
Information
A all downhill
B suitable for beginners
C only in good weather
D food included
E no charge
F swimming possible
G fully booked today
H transport not included

questions.content:
(11) dolphin watching
(12) forest walk
(13) cycle trip
(14) local craft tour
(15) observatory trip
(16) horse riding
options: A all downhill
B suitable for beginners
C only in good weather
D food included
E no charge
F swimming possible
G fully booked today

sections.instruction: Choose TWO letters A-E. Which TWO things does the speaker say about the attraction called Musical Favourites?

questions.content: Which TWO things does the speaker say about the attraction called Musical Favourites?
options: A You pay extra for drinks.
B You must book it in advance.
C You get a reduction if you buy two tickets.
D You can meet the performers.
E You can take part in the show.

sections.instruction: Choose TWO letters A-E. Which TWO things does the speaker say about the Castle Feast?

questions.content: Which TWO things does the speaker say about the Castle Feast?
options: A Visitors can dance after the meal.
B There is a choice of food.
C Visitors wear historical costume.
D Knives and forks are not used.
E The entertainment includes horse races.

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: What does Trevor find interesting about the purpose of children’s literature?
options: A the fact that authors may not realise what values they’re teaching
B the fact that literature can be entertaining and educational at the same time
C the fact that adults expect children to imitate characters in literature

questions.content: Trevor says the module about the purpose of children’s literature made him
options: A analyse some of the stories that his niece reads.
B wonder how far popularity reflects good quality.
C decide to start writing some children’s stories.

questions.content: Stephanie is interested in the Pictures module because
options: A she intends to become an illustrator.
B she can remember beautiful illustrations from her childhood.
C she believes illustrations are more important than words.

questions.content: Trevor and Stephanie agree that comics
options: A are inferior to books.
B have the potential for being useful.
C discourage children from using their imagination.

questions.content: With regard to books aimed at only boys or only girls, Trevor was surprised
options: A how long the distinction had gone unquestioned.
B how few books were aimed at both girls and boys.
C how many children enjoyed books intended for the opposite sex.

sections.instruction: What comment is made about each of these stories? Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 26-30.
sections.passage_or_context:
Comments
A translated into many other languages
B hard to read
C inspired a work in a different area of art
D more popular than the author’s other works
E original title refers to another book
F started a new genre
G unlikely topic

questions.content:
(26) Perrault’s fairy tales
(27) The Swiss Family Robinson
(28) The Nutcracker and The Mouse King
(29) The Lord of the Rings
(30) War Horse
options: A translated into many other languages
B hard to read
C inspired a work in a different area of art
D more popular than the author’s other works
E original title refers to another book
F started a new genre
G unlikely topic

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
The hunt for sunken settlements and ancient shipwrecks
ATLIT-YAM
• was a village on coast of eastern Mediterranean
• thrived until about 7,000 BC
• stone homes had a courtyard
• had a semicircle of large stones round a (31) {{BLANK}}
• cause of destruction unknown – now under the sea
• biggest settlement from the prehistoric period found on the seabed
• research carried out into structures, (32) {{BLANK}} and human remains
TRADITIONAL AUTONOMOUS UNDERWATER VEHICLES (AUVs)
• used in the oil industry, e.g. to make (33) {{BLANK}}
• problems: they were expensive and (34) {{BLANK}}
LATEST AUVs
• much easier to use, relatively cheap, sophisticated
Tests:
• Marzamemi, Sicily: found ancient Roman ships carrying architectural elements made of (35) {{BLANK}}
Underwater internet:
• (36) {{BLANK}} is used for short distance communication, acoustic waves for long distance
• plans for communication with researchers by satellite
• AUV can send data to another AUV that has better (37) {{BLANK}}, for example
Planned research in Gulf of Baratti:
• to find out more about wrecks of ancient Roman ships, including
– one carrying (38) {{BLANK}} supplies; tablets may have been used for cleaning the (39) {{BLANK}}
– others carrying containers of olive oil or (40) {{BLANK}}

questions.content:
The hunt for sunken settlements and ancient shipwrecks
ATLIT-YAM
• was a village on coast of eastern Mediterranean
• thrived until about 7,000 BC
• stone homes had a courtyard
• had a semicircle of large stones round a (31) {{BLANK}}
• cause of destruction unknown – now under the sea
• biggest settlement from the prehistoric period found on the seabed
• research carried out into structures, (32) {{BLANK}} and human remains
TRADITIONAL AUTONOMOUS UNDERWATER VEHICLES (AUVs)
• used in the oil industry, e.g. to make (33) {{BLANK}}
• problems: they were expensive and (34) {{BLANK}}
LATEST AUVs
• much easier to use, relatively cheap, sophisticated
Tests:
• Marzamemi, Sicily: found ancient Roman ships carrying architectural elements made of (35) {{BLANK}}
Underwater internet:
• (36) {{BLANK}} is used for short distance communication, acoustic waves for long distance
• plans for communication with researchers by satellite
• AUV can send data to another AUV that has better (37) {{BLANK}}, for example
Planned research in Gulf of Baratti:
• to find out more about wrecks of ancient Roman ships, including
– one carrying (38) {{BLANK}} supplies; tablets may have been used for cleaning the (39) {{BLANK}}
– others carrying containers of olive oil or (40) {{BLANK}}
